howItWorks.intro: This oil price recession probability calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Year-over-year change = (current - year ago) / year ago × 100
Probability based on historical correlation of oil % change to recession frequency
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How often do oil spikes cause recessions?
faq.answer: Since 1973, 6 of 7 US recessions were preceded by significant oil price increases. The exception was the 2020 COVID recession which was pandemic-driven. When oil doubles year-over-year, recession has followed within 18 months in 85% of cases.

faq.question: Why does oil price affect the whole economy?
faq.answer: Oil is embedded in transportation (70% of oil use), manufacturing, agriculture, and heating. When oil spikes, it acts as a tax on consumers and businesses, reducing spending power across the entire economy.
